title: Living in Boulder vlog 12 -  8 -  2017: Roman Roads on Google Earth

enrichment.summary: Kyle gives a late-night vlog update on his ongoing project of recreating ancient Rome in Google Earth, describing the painstaking process of pasting road images, deciding which roads to include based on historical maps, and planning next steps for adding buildings based on density data from a source called Platinum Ash Being. He also mentions visiting his mom's house to help with chores.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: slow but making progress; thinks he can finish roads the next day
prefers the Plataean/Ash Being figures and wants to make Region 9 consistent at exactly 233 apartment buildings and 140 houses
believes it will be amazing to walk around ancient Rome in ground view once complete; worth doing even just for himself
worried his computer may struggle with the large file but hopeful, and confident next-gen computers will handle it

entities: Platinum Ash Being (source/website on Roman buildings)

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I went to my mom's house and shampooed her house and helped organize the basement.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I've been working on a project involving roads and images, pasting little images of roads and cutting them to the right size.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I can't just cut and paste the road images because I can't move them for some reason.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I've built an earth boulder but didn't have a dirt road or something you can see moving under.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I have to design the roads from scratch, deciding which ones to include and leave out to make it consistent with the past.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm working on a map of Rome in 2011, including old roads and a lake made for a sea battle in Amasia.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I've been listening to Platinum and Ash, which mentioned there were 233 apartment buildings in Region 9 of Rome.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Region 9 is the oldest part of Rome that existed in the middle ages, north of downtown.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Region 9 has 140 houses, making it a lot less dense compared to Region 4, which was supposed to have 63,500 apartment buildings.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I might focus on Region 9 and trace all the buildings there to make it consistent with historical data.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Creating a detailed map of Rome with all buildings might cause problems because the file could be too big for my computer to handle.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I might partition the file or do screen captures of certain angles to manage the size.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I believe future computers will be able to handle the detailed map of Rome in Google Earth.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Seeing the cartoon world of Rome in Google Earth with labeled buildings and hills will be amazing.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I think I can finish the roads tomorrow and then start working on the buildings.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I've finished tracing all of the map and now need to give the buildings proper heights and colors.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I might be closer than I think to finishing the project.
